refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained a phrase 'meet or exceed' which can be simplified to 'achieve' to keep it clear and atomic. The FR focuses solely on the required operational availability metric, avoiding any implementation details or how the availability is achieved, thus maintaining solution neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design for high reliability components and subsystems to minimize failure rates
Implement real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Incorporate redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain operation during faults
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to support availability targets
Design modular system architecture to isolate faults and enable quick recovery
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incorporate redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain operation during faults

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms may interact with monitoring systems
Failover logic could be coupled with operational procedures
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical independence by isolating fault handling through redundancy and failover, which supports availability without tightly coupling to other DPs. Care should be taken to maintain clear interfaces between redundancy and monitoring or operational policies.

assumptions_risks: Availability target of ≥0.97 is achievable with redundancy alone
Operational policies are effectively implemented and adhered to
Redundancy adds complexity that may introduce new failure modes if not carefully managed
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time health monitoring as a complementary DP to enhance predictive maintenance and further improve availability assurance.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for system safety features including fault detection, redundancy, and failover mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or redundancy mechanisms are triggered

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and reports health metrics for monitoring redundancy and failover status

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules to coordinate failover and redundancy actions

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but diffusion is high due to many unrelated functions.
Physical system module; contains some redundancy but DP logic is more software/system safety oriented, causing diffusion.
Focused on remote control; redundancy/failover is peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Highly cohesive for DP; minimal diffusion as redundancy and failover are core safety functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing redundancy and failover mechanisms, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment of the DP.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling outside Safety Services.
If failover requires coordination with Vehicle hardware, some diffusion may occur.
Overlapping responsibilities with NGHE Solution could cause boundary ambiguity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine DP to explicitly include coordination protocols with Vehicle hardware and Teleoperation to clarify interface responsibilities and reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to incorporate redundancy and failover mechanisms directly addresses the FR to achieve the customer-defined operational availability target, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97 over the evaluation period.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling outside Safety Services.
If failover requires coordination with Vehicle hardware, some diffusion may occur.
Overlapping responsibilities with NGHE Solution could cause boundary ambiguity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion for implementing the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion and lower containment, supporting Safety Services as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring availability metrics (≥0.97) and ensuring operational policies and safety plans are followed, supported by Safety Services' interfaces for alerts and telemetry.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear with Safety Services owning the DP implementation, though coordination with Vehicle hardware and Teleoperation modules should be explicitly defined to avoid ambiguity.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly include coordination protocols with Vehicle hardware and Teleoperation modules to clarify interface responsibilities and reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear alignment to acceptance criteria, strong containment in the chosen IM boundary (Safety Services), and feasible verification methods. Although some diffusion risks and coordination ambiguities exist, these are manageable through recommended refinements rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and cohesion at this level is preferable to avoid complexity and potential errors from deeper decomposition.

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly include coordination protocols with Vehicle hardware and Teleoperation modules to clarify interface responsibilities and reduce diffusion risk.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR with redundancy and failover mechanisms.
Safety Services module is the optimal IM boundary based on containment and diffusion scores.
Verification through availability metrics and operational policies is feasible and reliable.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry, Network Link, Vehicle hardware, and NGHE Solution causing diffusion and boundary ambiguity.
Coordination protocols between Safety Services and other modules are not fully defined, risking interface issues.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications and coordination protocols between Safety Services, Vehicle hardware, and Teleoperation modules.
Verification data confirming availability ≥0.97 over evaluation period under operational conditions.
